methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $180M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $2.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: KoBold Metals
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $2B+

private.name: Electric Hydrogen
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B+

private.name: Amogy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $700M

data.narrative:
Zanskar, a Series C climate and energy company founded by Carl Hoiland and Joel Edwards, is evaluated against a comparables universe of 9 private peers and 1 public company. The three most recent private comps—KoBold Metals ($2.0B), Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B), and Amogy ($700M)—span a valuation range of $700M to $2.0B at the same stage.

Two valuation methods are applied. The stage-progression method uses total raised ($180M) as a proxy base and applies a 1.7x Series C step-up multiplier, yielding $306M. The peer-median method calculates the median valuation across the three same-sector, same-stage comparables at $1.0B, with a range floor of $700M and ceiling of $2.0B.

These methods triangulate to a low of $260M, median of $653M, and high of $1.1B. The wide spread reflects material dispersion in peer valuations and the reliance on a limited three-company peer set. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the peer cohort for Zanskar's specific subsector within climate tech, execution risk relative to stage, and whether the public comparable (LNZA) offers material validation. The $653M median sits between the stage-progression floor and the peer median, though this range warrants sensitivity analysis around peer selection and revenue/traction visibility.
